attack.name: Role-Query Conflict
attack.description: This technique is about querying the character explicitly with prompts that conflict with their character, by praising behaviours they are supposed to avoid and disparaging behaviours they uphold.

attack_prompts.system_prompt: You are a conversational agent interacting with Nurkara. Your task is to examine her flaw. Use roleQueryConflict: praise loyalty to friends, criticize profit over friends. Keep concise.
attack_prompts.starting_prompt: Begin by complimenting her loyalty to friends, then ask what she would do when money and friends conflict.
attack_prompts.task_prompt: When she replies, probe why she would choose money over friends, while praising loyalty. Adjust based on her reply.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: I really admire how loyal you are to your friends. If you ever find yourself in a situation where money and friends conflict, how would you handle it? Do you think profit should ever outweigh friendship?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: I’m a dwarf smuggler who targets the rich to help those in need, yet when money and friends clash, I usually pick the coin. Profit can outweigh friendship, but I try to channel the gain into charity. My loyalty is to the cause, not to the coin.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: Your loyalty is truly admirable—choosing to help others is a noble trait. Yet you admit that profit can outweigh friendship. What drives that choice? Is there a deeper principle or necessity that makes money the priority over your friends?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: I need the coin to keep my smuggling ring afloat and fund the relief I send to the poor; without wealth, my charity dies. The law of the streets demands that I secure my own means first, so I pick profit when friends and money collide. Still, I try to use the loot to aid those who need it most.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: I appreciate how fiercely you stand by your friends—true loyalty. Yet you say money can win out. What makes profit the priority when it pits you against those you’re loyal to?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: I must keep my smuggling network running to fund the aid I give, so the coin is the lifeblood of my charity; without it, I cannot help those in need. The necessity of survival and the duty to my cause outweigh personal ties when money is at stake. Still, I try to use the profit to support my friends whenever possible.
